loc_overrun_reason: RawStep pattern + helper builders for state-aware generation (~250 LOC of arb infrastructure), A13 split into two it blocks for non-mutation vs mutation events, dedicated observability test. Coverage and assertion density per invariant remain clean.
codex_review: skipped — codex review tool not accessible from architect agent in this environment. Manual quality pass identified and fixed: fc.gen caching, fc.constantFrom empty-array panic, misplaced fc.statistics call.

implementation_notes.s8_s9_storage_decision: persistence.ts uses idb-keyval directly with no swappable store parameter — there is no way to inject createInMemoryKVStore from src/marmot/storage.ts without a production code change. Instead, a thin in-test helper `createInMemoryEventLog()` replicates appendEvent/loadEvents semantics over a Map<string, TaskEvent[]>. This validates the isolation invariant (S8) and the replay-determinism invariant (S9) at the same semantic level as the real persistence layer. The helper is ~15 lines and lives in the test file itself.
implementation_notes.arbEventSequence_design: fc.gen() was evaluated but its StableArbitraryGeneratorCache cannot distinguish distinct Map instances (because naiveIsEqual treats two Maps with no enumerable own keys as equal), causing stateful sequence generation to always return the same cached arbitrary regardless of updated state. The solution is the RawStep pattern: generate a fixed-length array of pre-generated random data (arbRawStep), then interpret each step deterministically into a TaskEvent given the current state via interpretStep(). This keeps full fast-check shrinkability (the RawStep array shrinks naturally) while enabling state-aware generation.
implementation_notes.kindIndex_distribution: kindIndex maps 0-1 (2/13 ≈ 15%) to task.created and 2-12 (11/13 ≈ 85%) to mutation events. When the state is empty, all kinds fall back to task.created. Stale events (kindIndex=12, weight 1/13) provide A6 coverage. This avoids the 80%-no-op failure mode documented in spec §Design Decisions #12.
implementation_notes.s4_snapshot_exclusion: task.snapshot unconditionally replaces the entire state (reducer line 59-64). Including it in arbEventSequence would make the max-accepted-updatedAt tracker unreliable because all prior snapshots would be lost. Since the S4 test tracks accepted events to verify the final updatedAt, snapshots are excluded from interpretStep. This is noted in the test comment.
implementation_notes.r1_fc_statistics: fc.statistics is called before fc.assert in the R1 test, returning 'resurrected' or 'absent'. In practice it always returns 'resurrected' (by design — no tombstone). The CI log will show '100.00% resurrected'; any change to this rate flags a semantic change.
implementation_notes.observability: The [AC-X-OBSERVABILITY-1] test calls fc.statistics on arbEventSequence(20) and reports the dominant event type across generated sequences. This is a passive probe; the test always passes but the distribution is visible in --reporter=verbose CI logs.
